content: All fields are generated with JSON tags in snake_case by default. Fields marked with the 'secret' tag are not marshaled to JSON.

- ID: Identity of Tenant // constructor input
- Name: string // constructor input
- Status: Tenant Status // constructor input
- CreatedAt: time.Time
+ ID: Identity of Tenant - New Identity
+ Name: string - constructor input
+ Status: Enumerated string of "active", "inactive", default("active")
+ CreatedAt: time.Time - Default: now
+ SecretAttribute: string - secret

- func NewTenant(id TenantID, name string, status TenantStatus) *Tenant {
-     return &Tenant{
-         ID:     id,
-         Name:   name,
-         Status: status,
-         // other fields set to zero value or handled separately
-     }
- }
+ // TenantStatus represents the status of a tenant.
+ type TenantStatus string
+ 
+ const(
+     // TenantStatusActive indicates the tenant is active.
+     TenantStatusActive TenantStatus = "active"
+     // TenantStatusInactive indicates the tenant is inactive.
+     TenantStatusInactive TenantStatus = "inactive"
+ )
+ 
+ // Tenant represents a logical customer or organization using the application.
+ type Tenant struct {
+     ID                  TenantID      `json:"id"` // Unique identifier for the tenant
+     Name                string        `json:"name"` // Tenant name
+     Status              TenantStatus  `json:"status"` // Status of the tenant (active or inactive)
+     CreatedAt           time.Time     `json:"created_at"` // Timestamp of tenant creation
+     SecretAttribute     string        `json:"-"` // Tenant Secret attribute
+ }
+ 
+ // NewTenant creates a new Tenant with the given name and status.
+ // If status is empty, it defaults to TenantStatusActive.
+ // Returns an error if ID generation fails.
+ func NewTenant(name string, status TenantStatus) (*Tenant, error) {
+     id, err := uuid.New()
+     if err != nil {
+         return nil, err
+     }
+     now := time.Now().UTC()
+     if status == "" {
+         status = TenantStatusActive
+     }
+     return &Tenant{
+         ID:     id,
+         Name:   name,
+         Status: status,
+         CreatedAt: now,
+     }, nil
+ }
Entity can be generated by list of field definition

By default, generated entity also automatically has following field: CreatedAt time.Time, with default value : now in construction time; UpdatedAt time.Time, with default value : now in construction time; DeletedAt time.Time, with default value : nil; IsDeleted bool, with default value : false

- All fields are generated with JSON tags in snake_case by default. Fields marked with the 'secret' tag are not marshaled to JSON.
+ - All fields are generated with JSON tags in snake_case by default. Fields marked with the 'secret' tag are not marshaled to JSON.

- Entity Definition also generates constructor with field with **constructor input** tags as args
+ - Entity Definition also generates constructor with field with **constructor input** tags as args
Automatic fields are set in the constructor automatically and are not passed as constructor parameters
Developers can override automatic fields by explicitly defining them in the field list (e.g., if different default values or field names are needed)

- ID: Identity of Tenant - New Identity
- Name: string - constructor input
- Status: Enumerated string of "active", "inactive", default("active")
- CreatedAt: time.Time - Default: now
- SecretAttribute: string - secret

+     DeletedAt           time.Time     `json:"-"` // Timestamp of tenant soft deletion

Fields described Validated as **something** have validate tag complied with github.com/go-playground/validator library.

Note: Validation is handled in the presentation layer (DTOs) using github.com/go-playground/validator. Domain entities focus purely on business logic without presentation concerns.
